title: CIL Geology — Full-length Mock 03
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Swachh Bharat Mission Phase 2 focuses on:
questions.solution: All of the above

questions.stem: At what rate CI, ₹5,000 becomes ₹6,655 in 2 years?

questions.stem: A sum amounts to ₹9,680 in 2 years at 10% CI. Sum:

questions.stem: Half of sum at 8% SI and half at 12% SI. Total interest in 3 years is ₹1,200. Sum:

questions.stem: A trader marks 30% above cost and allows 15% trade and 5% cash discount. Net profit:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A boy goes 6 km east, 8 km south. Distance from start:
questions.solution: 10 km

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Angle between hands at 3:45:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 15, Ramesh is 7th from left. Suresh is 9th from right. How many between them?
questions.solution: None

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 50 students, A is 18th from left and B is 22nd from right. How many between A and B if they are not overlapping?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: Students who solve more mock tests score higher in the actual exam. Conclusion I: Solving mocks improves exam performance. II: Students who score high solve more mocks.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A security code has 4 digits (0-9) with first digit not zero. No digit repeated. How many codes possible?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Sita walks 30 m north, 40 m east. Geeta walks 40 m south, 30 m east from same point. Distance between them:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 100 years have 24 leap years, the century is:
questions.solution: Not divisible by 400

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Six friends P,Q,R,S,T,U sit around a circle. P is between S and Q. R is opposite P. T is right of R. Who is left of U?
questions.solution: Cannot determine

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a class rank list, Raj is 5th from top. His rank from bottom is 12th. Number of students:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: Coal production has grown 8% this year while renewable energy grew 25%. Conclusion I: India is moving towards green energy. II: Coal production is declining in absolute terms.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A team has 8 cricketers. A playing XI must have at least 4 bowlers and 4 batsmen. From this team, if 5 are bowlers and 3 are batsmen, how many valid XIs?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person walks 20 m toward east, takes right turn 30 m, left turn 15 m, left turn 40 m. Distance from start:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A watch shows 8:20. If minute hand points east, hour hand points:
questions.solution: North-east

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A hexagonal table has 6 seats. P is opposite Q. R is between P and S. T is opposite R. U is adjacent to Q and T. Who is opposite S?
questions.solution: U

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Scores: P > Q, R < S, Q > S, T > P. Who scores highest?
questions.solution: T

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: Mining accidents have reduced by 40% after new safety guidelines. Conclusion I: Old safety guidelines were ineffective. II: New guidelines have improved safety.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A cube painted on all faces is cut into 125 equal cubes. How many have 2 faces painted?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All chairs are tables. All tables are furniture. Some furniture is wood. Conclusions: I. Some chairs are wood. II. All chairs are furniture.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a line, X is 12th from left. Y is 18th from right. If they swap, X becomes 20th from left. Total people:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: 8, 27, 125, 343, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If COAL is coded as 3-15-1-12, then INDIA = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: F is the brother of A. C is the daughter of A. D is the sister of F. E is the mother of D. How is E related to C?
questions.solution: Grandmother

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, CAT = 24, RAT = 39, BAT = 23, then MAT = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Each of the participants were given a certificate.
options: Every of the participants were given a certificate.
Each of the participants were given a certificate.
Each of the participants was given a certificate.
Each of the participant was given a certificate.
questions.solution: Each of the participants was given a certificate.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The new policy is ____ to improve efficiency.
questions.solution: designed

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
options: The sceneries of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains have been breathtaking.
questions.solution: The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government ____ the emergency protocol yesterday.
questions.solution: activated

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
options: The professor gave a one hour lecture.
The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
questions.solution: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The findings are ____ with previous research.
questions.solution: consistent

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He asked that whether I could help him.
options: He asked that whether I could help him.
He asked if whether I could help him.
He asked that I could help him.
He asked whether I could help him.
questions.solution: He asked whether I could help him.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The success of the mission ____ on teamwork.
questions.solution: depends

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither the manager nor his assistants was present.
options: Neither the manager nor his assistants are being present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants has been present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants is present.
questions.solution: Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He ____ the opportunity to study abroad.
questions.solution: seized

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is suffering with high fever.
options: He is suffering from high fever.
He is suffering of high fever.
He is suffering by high fever.
He is suffering with high fever.
questions.solution: He is suffering from high fever.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government must ____ steps to control pollution.
questions.solution: take

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He asked me to convey him my regards.
options: He asked that I convey him my regards.
He asked me to convey to him my regards.
He asked me to convey my regards to him.
He asked me to convey him my regards.
questions.solution: He asked me to convey to him my regards.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The situation is ____ by the delay in approvals.
questions.solution: exacerbated

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is suffering from fever since last week.
options: He suffers from fever since last week.
He has been suffering from fever since last week.
He was suffering from fever since last week.
He is suffering from fever since last week.
questions.solution: He has been suffering from fever since last week.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The court ____ the contract void.
questions.solution: declared

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The price of these goods have increased.
options: The price of these goods has increased.
The price of these goods are increased.
The prices of these goods has increased.
The price of these goods have increased.
questions.solution: The price of these goods has increased.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Coral reefs are among the most diverse ecosystems. Q: They provide habitat for numerous marine species. R: Climate change and ocean acidification threaten their survival. S: Conservation programmes are working to protect and restore reefs.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To spill the beans':
questions.solution: To reveal a secret

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of BENEVOLENCE:
questions.solution: Malevolence

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: One of the miners have been hospitalised.
options: One of the miners has been hospitalised.
One of the miners is being hospitalised.
One of the miners are hospitalised.
One of the miners were hospitalised.
questions.solution: One of the miners has been hospitalised.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government is ____ to attract investment.
questions.solution: seeking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on me for being late.
options: She was angry at me for being late.
She was angry upon me for being late.
She was angry on me for being late.
She was angry with me for being late.
questions.solution: She was angry with me for being late.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The CEO ____ the concerns raised by the employees.
questions.solution: addressed

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of MAGNANIMOUS:
questions.solution: Generous

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Well interference:
options: Multiple wells → cone superposition. Additional drawdown. Reduced yield
questions.solution: Interference: s_total = Σs_i. Spacing wells to minimize. Constant rate vs constant drawdown.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stereographic projection of crystals:
options: Great circles and poles. Wulff (equal-angle) net. Angle between crystal faces measured
questions.solution: Stereographic: 2D representation of 3D angles. Measured on goniometer. Rotation about N-S axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: We currently live in which geological era?
questions.solution: The Cenozoic ('age of mammals') is the present era, following the Mesozoic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Glossopteris flora: distribution in Gondwana:
options: Permian seed fern found in India, Australia, Antarctica, Africa, South America
questions.solution: Glossopteris: Gondwana indicator. Found in coal measures. Key evidence for continental drift.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Cenozoic Era is characterized by:
options: Mammal diversification and growth of ice sheets
questions.solution: Cenozoic: Age of Mammals. India-Asia collision (Himalaya), Antarctic glaciation, grassland expansion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ore deposit models: porphyry Cu-Mo vs porphyry Cu-Au:
options: Cu-Mo: I-type, oxidized. Cu-Au: magnetite-rich, reduced
questions.solution: Porphyry Cu-Mo: calc-alkaline, I-type. Cu-Au: more oxidized, magnetite-rich. Kalgoorlie type.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Stable isotope δ18O in marine carbonates reflects:
questions.solution: δ18O: temperature proxy. Higher δ18O = colder (or more ice). Also reflects seawater composition changes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granulite facies conditions:
options: T > 700°C, P 3-10 kbar. Anhydrous minerals (pyroxene, garnet, feldspar)
questions.solution: Granulite: dry conditions. H₂O absent. Pyroxene + garnet + feldspar. Lower crust.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Eclogite facies indicates:
questions.solution: Eclogite: omphacite + garnet. Very high P (>1.2 GPa), moderate to high T. Indicative of subduction zone depths.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cuddapah Basin:
options: Proterozoic (1.6-0.6 Ga). Unconformably overlies Dharwar. Papaghni + Chitravati + Nallamalai + Srisailam
questions.solution: Cuddapah: Chevron fold belt (Nallamalai). Pb-Zn at Zawar (Rajasthan). Uranium at Lambapur.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Inselberg is an erosional remnant composed of:
questions.solution: Inselberg: isolated hill rising from plain. Erosional remnant of resistant rock. Common in arid tropical regions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic hazard:
options: Ground motion prediction. PGA, response spectra. Site effects (liquefaction, amplification)
questions.solution: Seismic: active faults, historical seismicity. Zonation maps. Microzonation for critical projects.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vorticity analysis:
options: Porphyroclast rotation. Rigid grains in ductile matrix. Rotational vs non-rotational deformation
questions.solution: Vorticity number W_k: simple shear (1), pure shear (0), general (0-1). Hyperbolic flow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aeolian sand transport: saltation height:
questions.solution: Saltation: bouncing grains. Trajectory height <1m. Creep: surface rolling. Suspension: fine particles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assimilation and fractional crystallization (AFC):
options: Country rock assimilation + fractional crystallization. Combined effect. DePaolo equations
questions.solution: AFC: r = assimilation rate / crystallization rate. Trace element + isotope modeling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquitard:
options: Low permeability unit (silt, clay, shale). Poor yield, may store/transmit slowly
questions.solution: Aquitard: retards vertical flow. Leakage through aquitard → aquitard-aquifer system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system: hexagonal system has:
options: 3 equal axes in plane (120°) and 1 perpendicular axis
questions.solution: Hexagonal: a₁=a₂=a₃≠c, α=β=90°, γ=120°. 6-fold symmetry. Quartz, beryl.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mohr-Coulomb failure criterion: τ = c + σ_n·tan(φ). For c=10MPa, φ=30°, σ_n=50MPa:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mantle convection: evidence from seismic tomography:
options: Low-velocity anomalies = hot (upwelling). High-velocity = cold (subducted slab)
questions.solution: Seismic tomography: 3D velocity model. Low V = hot mantle. High V = cold subducted slab.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Differential thermal analysis detects:
options: Endothermic and exothermic reactions in minerals
questions.solution: DTA: measures temperature difference between sample and reference.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 12.5% of its original parent isotope (half-life 1.3 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 12.5% remaining ⇒ 3 half-lives ⇒ age = 3×1.3 = 3.9 Gyr.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Groundwater contamination plume migrates:
questions.solution: Plume: follows hydraulic gradient. Advection (bulk flow) + dispersion (spreading). Density effects if DNAPL/LNAPL.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 7 km/s and arrives 30 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 7×30 = 210 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Braided river:
options: Multiple channels, bars (mid-channel). High sediment load, variable discharge
questions.solution: Braided: bars (longitudinal, transverse, point). Channel bifurcation. High energy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Anorthosite massifs:
options: Proterozoic (1.3-1.0 Ga). Plagioclase-rich (>90%). Andesine-labradorite. Adirondacks, Rogaland
questions.solution: Anorthosite: Al-rich crustal source? Intrusion at deep crustal levels. Associated with rapakivi granite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Oxygen isotope δ¹⁸O in marine carbonates:
options: More negative (depleted) = warmer climate or freshwater input
questions.solution: δ¹⁸O: foraminifera. More ¹⁶O in warm periods (less ice). δ¹⁸O increase = cooling/ice growth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Evaporite sequence — ideal brine concentration:
options: Calcite → Gypsum → Halite → K-Mg salts (sylvite, carnallite). 20× seawater concentration for halite
questions.solution: Evaporite: restricted basin. Dead Sea, Great Salt Lake. Salt giants: Messinian (5.96-5.33 Ma).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Spectral signature of water:
options: High reflectance in blue, decreasing toward NIR
questions.solution: Water absorbs NIR strongly. Reflects blue-green.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 10% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Continental collision: India-Asia collision (~55 Ma):
options: Tethys ocean closed. Himalayan orogeny. Tibetan Plateau uplift
questions.solution: India: northward drift ~20 cm/yr. Collision ~55 Ma. Himalaya: MCT, MBT, MFT. Tibet: thickened crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mineral processing: flotation:
options: Selective attachment of mineral particles to air bubbles. Collectors, frothers, modifiers
questions.solution: Flotation: surface chemistry. Collector (xanthate for sulfides). Frother (pine oil). pH modifier.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Permeability:
options: Ability to transmit fluids. Depends on pore connectivity, not just porosity
questions.solution: Perm: k = Q·μ·L/(A·ΔP) (Darcy's law). Shale: high porosity, very low perm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Indian coal-bearing formations rank:
questions.solution: Raniganj: high rank, good coking. Barakar: good quality. Karharbari: early, lower rank.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pegmatite rare-element deposits: LCT type enriched in:
options: Li, Cs, Ta, Nb, Be, Rb
questions.solution: LCT: Li-Cs-Ta pegmatites. NYF: Nb-Y-F. Economic: spodumene (Li), columbite-tantalite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sill:
questions.solution: Sill: horizontal sheet. Examples: Palisades Sill, Whin Sill.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phase diagram: ternary (plagioclase-quartz-K-feldspar) for granitic rocks. Thermal valley:
options: Minimum melt composition near granite (600-700°C at 200MPa H₂O)
questions.solution: Ternary minimum: granitic minimum melt. Low melting point of felsic system. Explains granite abundance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Basalt: most common volcanic rock. Ocean floor composition:
questions.solution: MORB: tholeiitic. Depleted mantle source. Low K2O, TiO2. Pillow lavas on ocean floor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Trace element discrimination diagram for tectonic setting: TiO₂ vs Zr for basalts. MORB:
questions.solution: MORB: depleted. OIB: enriched (high Ti, Zr). Island arc: low Ti, variable Zr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geological Time Scale:
options: Precambrian (88%), Paleozoic (545-251 Ma), Mesozoic (251-66 Ma), Cenozoic (66 Ma-present)
questions.solution: Hadean, Archean, Proterozoic (Precambrian). Phanerozoic: Paleo-Meso-Cenozoic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Folk limestone classification:
options: Allochens (grains) + orthocherms (matrix/cement). Skeletal, ooids, peloids, intraclasts
questions.solution: Folk: Sparry calcite cement = sparry allochemical. Micrite = microcrystalline ooze.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pseudotachylite:
options: Friction melt along fault. Glassy/aphantic. Co-seismic melting
questions.solution: Pseudotachylite: rapid frictional heating. Pseudotachylite veins. Vredefort impact structure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical resistivity tomography (ERT):
options: 2D/3D resistivity imaging. Dipole-dipole, Wenner, Schlumberger arrays. For contamination, cavity, archaeology
questions.solution: ERT: multi-electrode. Pseudo-section. Inversion. Time-lapse ERT for process monitoring.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porphyry copper — alteration zones:
options: Potassic (center) → phyllic → argillic → propylitic (outer). Sericite, chlorite, clay
questions.solution: Porphyry: Cu-Mo. Low grade (0.3-1% Cu). Large tonnage. Stockwork veins. Chile, USA, Indonesia.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Radiometric dating works because a radioactive parent isotope decays to a daughter at a rate set by its:
questions.solution: Decay is exponential with a fixed half-life, so the parent/daughter ratio gives an absolute age.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A dipping coal seam outcrops over a horizontal width of 50 m on flat ground and dips at 35°. Its true thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = horizontal width × sin(dip) = 50×sin35° ≈ 28.68 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 1 km², is 2 m thick, with density 1.4 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 1×10⁶ m²×2 m×1.4 t/m³ ≈ 2.8 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquifer definition:
options: Saturated permeable unit that yields useful water quantity
questions.solution: Aquifer: sand/gravel, sandstone, fractured limestone, basalt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Specific gravity of barite:
questions.solution: Barite (BaSO4) — high SG. Used as drilling mud additive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coastal landforms:
options: Cliffs, wave-cut platform, sea stack, sea arch, beach, bar, lagoon
questions.solution: Coastal: erosion (cliffs, stacks) and deposition (beaches, bars, spits).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geometric correction:
options: Remove geometric distortions. Map projection. GCPs. RMSE < 1 pixel. Orthorectification
questions.solution: Geometric: model sensor + terrain. GCP: ground control points. Resampling: nearest, bilinear, cubic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geosynthetics in slope stabilization: geogrids provide:
questions.solution: Geogrids: high tensile strength, interlock with soil. Geotextiles: filtration, separation. Geomembranes: waterproofing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Trachytic texture:
options: Aligned feldspar microlites in volcanic rock. Flow alignment
questions.solution: Trachytic: parallel alignment of plagioclase. Indicates flow direction in lava.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The law of superposition states that, in an undisturbed sequence, the oldest layer is:
questions.solution: Sediments are deposited oldest-first, so the lowest undisturbed bed is the oldest.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Storm-dominated shelf facies model:
options: Fair-weather wave base divides upper shoreface (high energy) from lower shoreface (low energy)
questions.solution: Upper: cross-bedded sand. Lower: hummocky cross-stratification (HCS). Storm deposits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mylonite:
options: Fine-grained, foliated rock in ductile shear zone. Grain size reduction by dynamic recrystallization
questions.solution: Mylonite: ductile deformation. Porphyroclasts in fine matrix. S-C fabrics. Protomylonite→mylonite→ultramylonite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a station the S-wave arrives 5 s after the P-wave. Using ~8 km per second of S–P delay, the epicentral distance is about:
questions.solution: distance ≈ (S−P time) × 8 km/s = 5×8 = 40 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 1 Mt has an average metal grade of 1%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 1 Mt × 1% = 10 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: (U-Th)/He dating:
options: ⁴He from α decay. Low T sensitivity (apatite ~70°C). Provided for exhumation/burial history
questions.solution: (U-Th)/He: thermochronology. He diffusion rate. Apatite (AHe), zircon (ZHe).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 20 m over a horizontal distance of 20 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(20/20) ≈ 45°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 20 m of overburden above a 5 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 20/5 = 4 : 1.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 20 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 5 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock sample of 100 cm³ contains 20 cm³ of pore space. Its porosity is:
questions.solution: Porosity = (void volume/total volume)×100 = 20/100 = 20%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Extinction — undulose in quartz:
questions.solution: Undulose extinction: common in deformed quartz. Indicates stress history.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Multispectral vs hyperspectral:
options: Hyperspectral has many narrow contiguous bands
questions.solution: Multispectral: 3-10 broad bands. Hyperspectral: 100+ narrow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Texture of komatiite:
questions.solution: Komatiite: Archean ultramafic volcanic. Spinifex due to rapid cooling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Graded bedding:
options: Coarsest at bottom, fining upward (turbidity current)
questions.solution: Graded: Bouma sequence in turbidites. Normal (fining up) or inverse (coarsening up).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic reflection: CMP (common midpoint) stacking improves:
options: Signal-to-noise ratio by factor of √n (n=stacking fold)
questions.solution: CMP: multiple traces with same midpoint. Stack: improves S/N. NMO correction before stacking.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 1 km², is 5 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 1×10⁶ m²×5 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 6.5 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Knickpoint:
options: Break in river profile (waterfall). Caused by base level fall, resistant rock, fault
questions.solution: Knickpoint: migrates upstream as river adjusts to new base level. Rejuvenation head.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carbonatite: igneous rock with >50% carbonate minerals. Key economic minerals:
questions.solution: Carbonatite: Oldoinyo Lengai (Tanzania). Only active carbonatite volcano. REE deposits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Nonconformity:
questions.solution: Nonconformity: erosion of basement + deposition. Basal conglomerate common.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Watershed delineation (GIS):
options: DEM → fill → flow direction → flow accumulation → pour point → watershed. Arc Hydro, TauDEM
questions.solution: Watershed: area draining to outlet. Sub-basins: stream ordering (Strahler, Shreve). Topographic wetness index.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fracture in rock along which there has been observable displacement is a:
questions.solution: Faults show relative movement of the two blocks; joints show none.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: LiDAR provides:
questions.solution: LiDAR: laser pulses measure distance. Creates DEMs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pumping test analysis: Theis equation s = Q·W(u)/(4πT). W(u) is:
questions.solution: W(u) = -0.5772-ln(u)+u-u²/... (series). u = r²S/(4Tt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Artificial recharge methods:
options: Check dams, recharge wells, percolation tanks, roof-top harvesting, injection wells
questions.solution: MAR: aquifer storage and recovery (ASR). Water quality treatment. Clogging management.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cleavage in mica:
questions.solution: Mica splits into thin sheets. Muscovite (white) and biotite (black).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 10% moisture, 35% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 5% moisture, 35% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (5+35+10) = 50%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 20 m over a horizontal distance of 40 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(20/40) ≈ 26.6°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Specific gravity of quartz:
questions.solution: Quartz SG ~2.65. Heavy minerals have SG > 2.85.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater in alluvium:
options: Porous, high storage. Sustainable yields. River recharge
questions.solution: Alluvium: sand/gravel aquifers. Good quality. Near-river zones have high recharge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 2 km², is 2 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 2×10⁶ m²×2 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 5.2 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 60° shows a width of 40 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 40×sin60° ≈ 34.64 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rslickenline (striae on fault surface) indicates:
options: Direction of last movement on fault
questions.solution: Slickenlines: linear features on fault surface. Mineral fibers grow in slip direction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stock:
questions.solution: Stock: similar to batholith but smaller. Often associated with mineral deposits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shear sense indicators — sigma (σ) porphyroclast:
options: Tails at angle to foliation. σ_a (asymmetric tails on both sides) and σ_c (tails in pressure shadow)
questions.solution: σ: tails parallel to shear direction. σ-type: tails merge in matrix. δ-type: tails embayed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydrologic cycle components:
options: Precipitation, evapotranspiration, runoff, infiltration, percolation, groundwater flow
questions.solution: Water balance: P = R + ET + ΔS. Global: 70% evapotranspiration, 30% runoff.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydraulic testing: packer tests isolate:
options: Specific depth intervals in borehole for permeability measurement
questions.solution: Packer test: inflatable packers seal interval. Pump or slug test in isolated zone.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: NDVI uses:
questions.solution: NDVI = (NIR - Red)/(NIR + Red). High = healthy vegetation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral that effervesces (fizzes) when dilute hydrochloric acid is applied is:
questions.solution: Calcite (CaCO₃) reacts with dilute HCl, releasing CO₂ — a standard field test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A good index (guide) fossil should be:
options: Widespread but short-lived in geologic time
questions.solution: Wide distribution plus a short time range makes a fossil useful for correlation/dating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Evaporation measurement — pan:
options: Class A evaporation pan. Pan coefficient (0.7-0.8) to convert to lake evaporation
questions.solution: Class A pan: 1.2m diameter, 0.25m deep. Pan evaporation > lake evaporation (K_p).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spaceborne sensors — Sentinel-2:
options: MSI (13 bands, 10-60m). 5-day revisit. 290km swath. Vegetation, agriculture

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Twinning in plagioclase:
questions.solution: Polysynthetic twinning diagnostic of plagioclase under microscope.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reverse/thrust fault:
options: Hanging wall moves up (compression). Thrust: <45° dip
questions.solution: Reverse: shortening. Thrust: low angle (<45°). Nappes: large displacement thrusts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 15 m³ of coal, 45 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 45/15 = 3 : 1.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Thermal infrared remote sensing measures:
questions.solution: TIR: 8-14 um wavelength. Detects emitted thermal radiation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Solifluction:
options: Slow downslope flow of water-saturated soil over permafrost. Gelifluction
questions.solution: Solifluction: lobes, sheets. 1-10 cm/yr. Gelifluction: thaw of ice lenses. Terrace-like landforms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ignimbrite (welded tuff):
options: Pyroclastic flow deposit. Welded: glass shards fused at high temperature
questions.solution: Ignimbrite: welded pyroclastic. High temperature (>600°C) → welding. Devitrification of glass shards.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Crystal system with 6-fold symmetry axis is:
questions.solution: Hexagonal: one 6-fold axis.
